metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the `rg -c ... == 0` proving command with `! rg 'Gerrit|module-size|Serena' templates/AGENTS.md` (exits 0 iff no match) to make the absence-check reliable and copy-runnable.
evidence: ripgrep --count prints nothing and exits 1 on zero matches, not '0' + exit 0
Defect taxonomy type 4: fixture/command does not actually exercise the claim as described
findings.finding: The proving command `rg -c 'Gerrit|module-size|Serena' templates/AGENTS.md` == 0 is a verify-command defect: ripgrep exits with code 1 (not code 0 printing '0') when no lines match, so the shell comparison `== 0` against exit code or output is undefined/unreliable as written.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC1 Proving line: '`rg -c 'Gerrit|module-size|Serena' templates/AGENTS.md` == 0'
scenarios: A CI script that runs the command literally will always exit 1 (never match exists), masking the intended absence-check; a naive `[ $(rg -c ...) -eq 0 ]` will error because rg exits non-zero before the shell comparison runs.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the proving line to: `! rg 'Gerrit|module-size|Serena' templates/AGENTS.md` (exits 0 when no forbidden terms are found, exits 1 when any are present).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit AC (and corresponding test branch) asserting that the drift-guard raises an error (not a vacuous pass) when the BEGIN/END delimiter markers are absent or malformed in either the template or the canonical source — preventing the empty-vs-empty tautology.
evidence: Plan specifies region extraction by `<!-- BEGIN rebar-usage (generated; do not edit) -->` / `<!-- END rebar-usage -->` markers
No AC or test branch specified for the case where markers are absent/malformed in the template
Existing `test_divergence_is_detected` only covers 'perturbing the delimited block', not removing/corrupting the delimiters themselves
An extractor returning `''` on missing markers would make an empty-vs-empty comparison pass vacuously
findings.finding: The drift-guard region-extractor has no specified behavior when the BEGIN/END delimiter markers are absent or malformed in the template. If the extractor silently returns an empty string, it would compare empty-to-empty (or empty-to-canonical), producing a vacuous pass — the test goes green while the actual sync guard is completely broken. This is the self-authored-oracle / tautology anti-pattern: the test locks in a broken state without detection.
findings.location: De-risking results section / Revised AC: 'The drift-guard test compares the DELIMITED rebar-usage region'
scenarios: Developer accidentally deletes the BEGIN/END markers while editing the template — drift-guard passes vacuously, believing the region is in sync
A refactor renames the markers in the canonical source but not the template — extractor returns empty on one side, comparison result depends on implementation; silent pass is possible
findings.suggested_fix: In `test_client_template_sync.py`, after extracting the region, assert `len(region) > 0` (or that the extractor raises `ValueError` on missing markers) before the byte-identical comparison. Add a `test_missing_markers_raises` test case that removes the markers from a temp copy and asserts the extractor raises rather than returning empty.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the drift-guard test must assert the delimited block is non-empty and that exactly one BEGIN/END delimiter pair is present, failing loudly (not returning an empty string) when delimiters are absent or malformed.
evidence: Plan text: 'embed it in templates/AGENTS.md between <!-- BEGIN rebar-usage (generated; do not edit) --> / <!-- END rebar-usage -->'
Plan text: 'the new test asserts that delimited block is byte-identical to the canonical source' — no behavior specified for absent/malformed delimiters
test_skill_shared_sync.py _identical(): 'return copy.is_file() and copy.read_bytes() == canonical.read_bytes()' — whole-file, no extraction edge case
ABSENCE: no failing behavior for missing/duplicated delimiter markers is mentioned anywhere in the plan
findings.finding: The drift-guard region-extractor has no specified behavior when the BEGIN/END delimiter markers are absent, appear in the wrong order, or appear more than once in the template. Unlike the whole-file `_identical` check in test_skill_shared_sync.py (which trivially handles missing files), a region-extractor that silently returns an empty string when delimiters are missing would compare '' == '' and vacuously pass — meaning the guard silently stops guarding the moment the delimiters are accidentally deleted. This is a mechanism correctness gap (the guard can be defeated by the very drift it's meant to catch).
findings.location: De-risking results section: 'the new test asserts that delimited block is byte-identical to the canonical source (mirroring _identical + a divergence self-check)'
scenarios: A developer refactors the template header and accidentally deletes the BEGIN comment; the extractor returns ''; '' == '' passes; drift is introduced invisibly
The template is copied by a client who strips HTML comments; the test in the rebar repo continues to pass against the now-empty extraction
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Revised AC: 'The region extractor raises an error (causing the test to fail) if the BEGIN or END delimiter is absent or appears more than once in the template, so delimiter deletion is itself caught by the gate.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define and document the UPDATE path for the delimited rebar-usage region (e.g., a `make sync-template` target or explicit in-test error message) so that when the canonical source changes, developers have an authoritative, low-friction procedure to re-sync the block in `templates/AGENTS.md`.
evidence: 'embed it in templates/AGENTS.md between <!-- BEGIN rebar-usage (generated; do not edit) --> / <!-- END rebar-usage -->'
'the new test asserts that delimited block is byte-identical to the canonical source'
ABSENCE: no mention of a sync script, Makefile target, or manual copy instruction when the canonical source changes
findings.finding: The plan specifies a drift-guard test that detects when the delimited rebar-usage region diverges from the canonical source, but does not define HOW a developer should regenerate/re-sync that block when the canonical source legitimately changes. Without a documented update procedure (e.g., a `make sync-template` target or an in-test instruction), the UPDATE ownership for the 'generated; do not edit' region is undefined — developers will see a red test but have no authoritative path to fix it.
findings.location: De-risking results section / Revised AC / templates/AGENTS.md delimited region design
scenarios: Developer updates `docs/ticket-model.md` (or `docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md`); `make test` turns red; developer does not know whether to manually copy the region, run a script, or use a generator — the plan provides no answer.
A new contributor sees 'do not edit' but the test is failing; they don't know the authoritative update path and may edit the canonical source back to match the stale template instead.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a Makefile target such as `sync-template` that copies the canonical region into the delimited block, and have the drift-guard test's failure message print: 'Run `make sync-template` to update the generated region.' Reference this target in the plan's AC proving steps.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the extraction protocol's behavior when BEGIN/END delimiters are missing or malformed: the test should assert that both markers are present (raising a clear error if not) before extracting the region, so delimiter deletion is itself a test failure.
evidence: Plan specifies delimiter markers but gives no rule for what the test does when markers are missing or appear multiple times
The existing test_skill_shared_sync.py `_identical` helper does `copy.read_bytes() == canonical.read_bytes()` — no parsing — so there is no pattern to extend
No AC or prose addresses the 'missing delimiter' case
findings.finding: The delimiter-extraction protocol for the drift-guard test specifies no fallback behavior when the BEGIN/END markers (`<!-- BEGIN rebar-usage ... -->` / `<!-- END rebar-usage -->`) are absent or malformed in either `templates/AGENTS.md` or the canonical source. The referenced analog (`test_skill_shared_sync.py`) does whole-file byte comparison with no parsing, so there is no inherited behavior. A silent empty-string match would make the test pass vacuously when delimiters are deleted; a crash would give an unintelligible failure.
findings.location: De-risking results / Resolved Design section: 'embed it in templates/AGENTS.md between <!-- BEGIN rebar-usage (generated; do not edit) --> / <!-- END rebar-usage -->; the new test asserts that delimited block is byte-identical to the canonical source'
scenarios: A developer accidentally deletes the BEGIN/END markers while editing the template; the test extractor returns an empty string and compares it to an empty string (if canonical source also lacks markers), passing vacuously.
A developer adds a second BEGIN/END block; the extractor picks the wrong one, masking real drift.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the De-risking section: 'The extractor MUST assert both delimiter markers are present in both the template and the canonical source before comparing; a missing marker is treated as a test failure with a message identifying which file and which marker is absent.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the canonical source file as a firm decision (e.g. `docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md`) rather than 'natural candidate X or Y', so the executor can wire the test without a design choice mid-session.
evidence: 'natural candidate … or a dedicated docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md' — two options presented, no decision recorded
Absence of a firm file path for the canonical source
findings.finding: The canonical source for the delimited rebar-usage region is left as 'natural candidate: docs/ticket-model.md … or a dedicated docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md' — the executor must pick one without a stated default, risking divergent implementations or a mid-session design detour.
findings.location: De-risking results section: 'Keep the canonical portable rebar-usage prose in ONE source (natural candidate: docs/ticket-model.md plus the workflow loop authored in S1, or a dedicated docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md)'
scenarios: Executor creates docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md when the re-home story actually settled on docs/ticket-model.md (or vice versa), causing the drift-guard to compare against the wrong file and silently pass on a mis-sync.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'natural candidate: docs/ticket-model.md plus the workflow loop authored in S1, or a dedicated docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md' with a single committed path such as 'canonical source: `docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md` (created as part of this story if not already present from the re-home story)'.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strike or explicitly supersede the original AC 2 with the Revised AC 1 wording so only one drift-guard criterion exists and it unambiguously specifies delimited-region comparison.
evidence: Original AC 2: 'A drift-guard test fails when the template diverges from its authoritative source … deliberately editing the template makes the test fail'
Revised AC 1: 'The drift-guard test compares the DELIMITED rebar-usage region (not the whole file) and fails when it diverges from the canonical source; a self-test proves divergence is detected'
The original AC implies whole-file comparison; the revised AC explicitly restricts to a delimited region — these are contradictory scopes for the same test.
findings.finding: The original Acceptance Criteria section retains AC 2 ('A drift-guard test fails when the template diverges…; deliberately editing the template makes the test fail') alongside Revised AC 1 in the De-risking section ('The drift-guard test compares the DELIMITED rebar-usage region … a self-test proves divergence is detected'). Both specify the same drift-guard self-test requirement, creating a near-duplicate criterion with slightly different language — one is whole-file, the other region-scoped — which could send the implementer in two directions.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (AC 2) vs De-risking results (Revised AC 1)
scenarios: Implementer implements the delimited-region test (correct) but CI reviewer flags it as not meeting the original AC which says 'template diverges from its authoritative source' without the region qualifier.
A future maintainer reads only the original AC section and re-implements whole-file comparison, breaking the intended design.
findings.suggested_fix: Delete the original AC 2 bullet from the Acceptance Criteria section and keep only the Revised AC 1 bullet (from De-risking), or add an explicit '(superseded by Revised AC 1 below)' annotation on the original.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Pin the canonical source file for the delimited rebar-usage region (or make it an explicit blocking condition on the re-home story's completion) so the test implementation has a definitive coupling target rather than two 'natural candidates'.
evidence: 'Keep the canonical portable rebar-usage prose in ONE source (natural candidate: docs/ticket-model.md plus the workflow loop authored in S1, or a dedicated docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md)'
'Depends on: the re-home story (the portable rebar-usage content must be settled)' — acknowledged but the coupling target is not pinned.
Two candidate sources are listed without a decision criterion, leaving implementers to choose arbitrarily.
findings.finding: The canonical source file for the delimited rebar-usage region is left unresolved ('natural candidate: docs/ticket-model.md plus the workflow loop... or a dedicated docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md'), creating an ambiguous coupling point that the test implementation must resolve without plan-level specification.
findings.location: De-risking results section; Scope section ('Depends on: the re-home story')
scenarios: Implementer picks docs/ticket-model.md; a later re-home story moves the content to docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md — the test silently points at a stale or wrong file.
Two contributors independently implement adjacent tasks and choose different canonical sources, requiring a merge-time reconciliation.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving command for AC3, e.g. `grep -r 'templates/AGENTS.md' docs/` or `grep 'AGENTS.md' docs/index.md` (using the actual index file path), so the docs-index update is deterministically verifiable.
evidence: AC1 and AC2 each carry explicit 'Proving:' lines; AC3 carries none
Absence of a grep/test command against the docs index file
findings.finding: AC3 ('The docs index references the template so adopters can find it') has no proving command; an executor has no deterministic in-session check to verify this criterion is satisfied.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC3: 'The docs index references the template so adopters can find it.'
scenarios: Executor adds the template file but forgets the docs index update; CI passes because no check covers it; the criterion is silently unmet.
findings.suggested_fix: Add under AC3: 'Proving: `grep -l "templates/AGENTS.md" docs/` returns at least one file.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Pin the canonical source file path (e.g. `docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md`) in the Revised AC's verify step, OR gate the Revised AC on the re-home story completing first and mark it as a blocking dependency.
evidence: 'Depends on: the re-home story (the portable rebar-usage content must be settled)'
'natural candidate: docs/ticket-model.md plus the workflow loop authored in S1, or a dedicated docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md'
Revised AC: 'the drift-guard test compares the DELIMITED rebar-usage region … and fails when it diverges from the canonical source'
findings.finding: The Revised AC requires the drift-guard test compare the delimited block 'byte-identical to the canonical source', but the canonical source is left unresolved ('natural candidate: docs/ticket-model.md … or a dedicated docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md') and the Scope explicitly declares a dependency on 're-home story (the portable rebar-usage content must be settled)'. The AC's verify step references an entity that is not yet pinned, making the criterion unmeasurable until the dependency lands — yet the plan presents the Revised AC as ready to implement now.
findings.location: Scope (Depends on), De-risking results (Revised AC 1), Acceptance Criteria
scenarios: Implementer writes the test against docs/ticket-model.md; re-home story later places canonical prose in docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md — test is immediately broken and must be rewritten.
Reviewer tries to verify the AC but 'the canonical source' has no authoritative name to check against.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'natural candidate: docs/ticket-model.md … or a dedicated docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md' with a single definitive path (preferably the dedicated shared file), reference that exact path in the Revised AC verify step, and note that this story is blocked until that file is authored.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Pin the exact canonical source file path for the delimited rebar-usage region in the drift-guard AC (e.g., `docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md`), and gate this ticket on that file existing or add a sub-step to create it.
evidence: 'natural candidate: docs/ticket-model.md plus the workflow loop authored in S1, or a dedicated docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md'
'Depends on: the re-home story (the portable rebar-usage content must be settled)'
Revised AC references 'the canonical source' without naming it
findings.finding: The canonical source file for the delimited rebar-usage region is not definitively identified in the ACs — the plan lists 'natural candidates' (docs/ticket-model.md or docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md) and gates resolution on 'the re-home story', leaving the drift-guard AC without a fully observable proof tied to a specific file path.
findings.location: De-risking results section / Revised Acceptance Criteria: 'fails when it diverges from the canonical source'
scenarios: Implementer must guess which file is authoritative; if they choose wrong the drift-guard silently protects the wrong content
If the re-home story isn't resolved before this ticket is picked up, the drift-guard cannot be wired correctly and the AC cannot be verified
findings.suggested_fix: In the Revised Acceptance Criteria, replace 'the canonical source' with a concrete path such as `docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md` and add a dependency check: 'This file must exist and contain the settled portable rebar-usage prose before the drift-guard test is wired.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the canonical-source ambiguity: pin the plan to exactly ONE file (either `docs/ticket-model.md` from the depends_on ticket, or a new `docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md`) and add whichever is chosen to this ticket's file_impact so it is tracked as a deliverable.
evidence: Plan text: 'natural candidate: docs/ticket-model.md plus the workflow loop authored in S1, or a dedicated docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md'
ticket 073d-51d8-3e58-4b14 file_impact lists: docs/ticket-model.md, docs/event-schema.md, docs/concurrency.md, docs/README.md — no docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md
This ticket's file_impact lists: templates/AGENTS.md, tests/unit/test_client_template_sync.py, docs/README.md — no docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md
docs/_shared/ does not exist in the repo
findings.finding: If the executor chooses `docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md` as the canonical source (explicitly listed as a 'natural candidate' in the De-risking section), that file is not enumerated in this ticket's file_impact nor in the depends_on ticket's (073d-51d8-3e58-4b14) file_impact, making it an unenumerated edit target that the plan does not account for.
findings.location: De-risking results section: 'natural candidate: docs/ticket-model.md plus the workflow loop authored in S1, or a dedicated docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md'
scenarios: Executor picks docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md as the canonical source, creates it, but it appears in no file_impact list and is never reviewed/tracked as a deliverable.
Review of the change description misses the new canonical-source file because it is not enumerated.
findings.suggested_fix: Prefer `docs/ticket-model.md` (already created by 073d-51d8-3e58-4b14) as the canonical source; update the De-risking section to say definitively 'the canonical source is docs/ticket-model.md' and remove the 'or docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md' alternative.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Wire the forbidden-content check (`Gerrit`, `module-size`, `Serena` absent from `templates/AGENTS.md`) into the automated test suite (e.g., as an assertion in `test_client_template_sync.py`) so `make test` enforces it in CI.
evidence: Plan states the forbidden-content check as: '`rg -c \'Gerrit|module-size|Serena\' templates/AGENTS.md` == 0' — a manual proving command
The only test wired into `make test` is the drift-guard region-sync test; no automated assertion of the content-prohibition invariant is specified
Revised AC section does not mention automating the forbidden-content check
findings.finding: The AC that enforces 'no rebar-internal Gerrit/module-size/Serena content' in the template is proved only by a manual `rg` command and is not wired into the automated test suite or `make test`. A future edit adding forbidden content would not be caught by CI.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, first bullet: 'rg -c Gerrit|module-size|Serena templates/AGENTS.md == 0'
scenarios: A contributor adds a Gerrit-specific workflow note inside the placeholder region — CI stays green, the invariant is violated, adopters receive rebar-internal content they were explicitly meant not to see
findings.suggested_fix: Add a test function `test_no_rebar_internal_content()` in `test_client_template_sync.py` that reads `templates/AGENTS.md` and asserts none of the forbidden terms appear, mirroring the `rg` command as an in-process assertion.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Cite or add a spike result confirming which major agent harnesses (Claude Code, Codex/OpenAI, Gemini CLI, Cursor, Windsurf, etc.) auto-load a root-level `AGENTS.md` by default, so the 'one file serves all agents' claim is grounded before the template is shipped as the authoritative multi-provider solution.
evidence: Plan quote: 'AGENTS.md is read by every major harness, one file serves all their agents'
Repo uses CLAUDE.md as its own agent-guidance file (confirmed: CLAUDE.md exists at repo root with full agent guide content)
No citation to harness documentation or spike evidence in the plan for multi-harness AGENTS.md auto-loading
Absence: no AC proving command verifies cross-harness coverage (e.g. no link to Codex/Gemini/Cursor docs confirming they pick up AGENTS.md)
findings.finding: The plan's core value proposition — 'AGENTS.md is read by every major harness, one file serves all their agents' — is an unverified capability assertion. The repo's own agent guidance file is CLAUDE.md (confirmed in codebase). It is not established in the plan which harnesses (Codex/OpenAI, Gemini CLI, Cursor, Windsurf, etc.) actually auto-load a root-level AGENTS.md by default, versus requiring CLAUDE.md or a different convention. If a significant harness does not read AGENTS.md, the 'provider-neutral' claim breaks down and adopters who drop the template into their repo would not get the promised cross-provider coverage.
findings.location: Why section: 'because `AGENTS.md` is read by every major harness, one file serves all their agents'
scenarios: An adopter copies templates/AGENTS.md into their repo expecting their Cursor or Gemini CLI agent to auto-load it, but that harness uses a different convention — the 'one file serves all' promise is silently broken.
The drift-guard test and template ship correctly, but the template's placement and name only cover one or two harnesses, so the docs-index mention oversells provider neutrality to adopters.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Harness compatibility' note in the plan (or a link to verified harness docs) listing which providers read `AGENTS.md` and which require a different file (e.g. `CLAUDE.md` for Claude Code, if `@AGENTS.md` import is not used). If coverage is partial, scope the claim accordingly (e.g. 'serves Codex and Gemini; Claude Code users also need a `@AGENTS.md` import in CLAUDE.md') and update the AC proving commands to include a harness-coverage check.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the delimited-region drift-guard pattern as a documented convention (e.g., a note in CLAUDE.md or a comment block in the test file itself) so future contributors know when to use region-sync vs. whole-file-sync.
evidence: 'The existing tests/unit/test_skill_shared_sync.py compares whole files byte-for-byte... That pattern does NOT fit the template... Resolved design: sync a DELIMITED REGION, not the whole file.'
No mention of updating CLAUDE.md, adding an ADR, or annotating the test pattern anywhere in the plan.
Plan requires 'a short header comment' only on templates/AGENTS.md itself, not on the test's design rationale.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new drift-guard pattern (delimited-region sync) that differs from the existing whole-file pattern in test_skill_shared_sync.py, but does not propose capturing this architectural decision in CLAUDE.md or a design doc, leaving future contributors without guidance on which pattern to apply when adding similar tests.
findings.location: De-risking results section; What section ('Add a drift-guard test')
scenarios: A future contributor adds a new drift-guard test and copies the whole-file pattern from test_skill_shared_sync.py, not knowing the delimited-region pattern exists.
A reviewer questions why two different sync patterns are used across the test suite with no documented rationale.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a section to CLAUDE.md (or the test file's module docstring) explaining: 'Drift-guard tests use whole-file comparison (test_skill_shared_sync.py pattern) when the artifact is expected to be byte-identical to the source; use delimited-region comparison (test_client_template_sync.py pattern) when the artifact intentionally adds client-specific content around a canonical block.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify and commit (or confirm already-committed) the exact canonical source file path for the delimited rebar-usage region — `docs/ticket-model.md` is referenced in the plan but does not exist; the plan must name a concrete, existing (or co-shipped) file rather than 'natural candidate'.
evidence: docs/ directory listing contains no ticket-model.md
Plan quote: 'natural candidate: docs/ticket-model.md plus the workflow loop authored in S1, or a dedicated docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md'
Grep for 'docs/ticket-model.md' returns zero repo-file hits
Revised AC requires drift-guard to compare 'delimited block byte-identical to the canonical source' — canonical source is undefined without this file

An executor following this plan cannot implement the drift-guard test without a canonical source file, and the named file is absent.
findings.location: De-risking results section: 'natural candidate: docs/ticket-model.md plus the workflow loop authored in S1'
scenarios: Implementer writes the drift-guard test pointing at docs/ticket-model.md and the test immediately fails because the file does not exist, blocking make test from going green.
Implementer defers choosing a canonical source, leaving the AC unverifiable and the test unwritten.
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) create `docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md` as part of this plan's deliverables and hardcode that path in the drift-guard test, or (b) name an already-existing docs file (e.g. `docs/user-guide.md`) that already contains the portable workflow prose and extract the region from it. Add the chosen path to AC2's proving command.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm `docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md` either (a) will be co-created in this plan's scope (add it to the 'In:' scope list and add an AC proving it exists) or (b) strike it as a candidate and name the actual existing file the test will reference.
evidence: Directory listing of docs/ shows no _shared/ subdirectory
Grep for 'docs/_shared' returns zero repo-file hits
Plan frames this as an existing-or-to-be-created option without committing to creating it in this plan's scope
Scope section ('In:') lists only templates/AGENTS.md, one new test, and a one-line docs mention — docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md is not listed
findings.finding: `docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md` — the alternate 'natural candidate' canonical source named in the De-risking section — also does not exist; the `docs/_shared/` directory itself is absent from the repo. Both named candidates for the drift-guard's canonical source are unverifiable.
findings.location: De-risking results section: 'or a dedicated docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md'
scenarios: Executor picks docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md as the canonical source but finds neither the directory nor file exists, then must create both — but creating a new docs file is not in scope and has no AC.
The two ambiguous candidates leave the implementer choosing arbitrarily, breaking the plan's reproducibility.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the canonical source for the delimited rebar-usage region to a single, named file (e.g. commit to `docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md` as a new file this story creates, or name the specific section of an existing doc), and update the Revised AC's proving command to reference that exact path.
evidence: Plan text: 'Keep the canonical portable rebar-usage prose in ONE source (natural candidate: docs/ticket-model.md plus the workflow loop authored in S1, or a dedicated docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md)'
Neither docs/ticket-model.md nor docs/_shared/ exists in the repo (confirmed by directory listing and search)
Revised AC: 'The drift-guard test compares the DELIMITED rebar-usage region … fails when it diverges from the canonical source' — but canonical source is unnamed
ABSENCE: no file in docs/ matches 'ticket-model' or '_shared/rebar-usage'
findings.finding: The canonical source file for the delimited rebar-usage region is not resolved in the plan: it is described as a 'natural candidate' (docs/ticket-model.md or a dedicated docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md), but neither file exists in the repo today, and the plan does not commit to one. The drift-guard test's byte-identical comparison has no anchor until this is pinned, meaning the core mechanism cannot be fully implemented as written — the executor must make a design decision the plan left open.
findings.location: De-risking results section: 'natural candidate: docs/ticket-model.md plus the workflow loop authored in S1, or a dedicated docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md'
scenarios: Implementer creates docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md; a later story author writes docs/ticket-model.md as a different source — two sources diverge and neither test catches it
Implementer picks user-guide.md sections as the canonical source but the region boundaries don't match the planned dedicated file, producing a test that passes against the wrong thing
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Revised AC: 'Canonical source is `docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md` (created by this story); the drift-guard test reads that file and compares it byte-for-byte to the delimited block in `templates/AGENTS.md`.' This collapses the 'natural candidate' ambiguity into a concrete deliverable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Pin the canonical source file path for the delimited rebar-usage region (e.g. `docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md` or `docs/ticket-model.md`) as a hard deliverable in the plan, confirm it either exists or is created as part of this story, and reference it by exact path in both the Acceptance Criteria and the drift-guard test description.
evidence: Plan text: 'natural candidate: docs/ticket-model.md plus the workflow loop authored in S1, or a dedicated docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md'
Search for 'ticket-model' in docs/ returns no file named ticket-model.md — the file does not exist
Search for '_shared' in docs/ returns no directory or file matching docs/_shared/
Revised AC: 'the drift-guard test compares the DELIMITED rebar-usage region ... byte-identical to the canonical source' — but canonical source path is never pinned
findings.finding: The canonical source file for the delimited rebar-usage region — the file the drift-guard test compares against — is never definitively resolved. The plan offers 'natural candidate: docs/ticket-model.md plus the workflow loop authored in S1, or a dedicated docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md', but neither file exists in the repository (confirmed by search). An executor implementing the test cannot wire up the path constant without making an arbitrary choice not sanctioned by the plan.
findings.location: De-risking results / Resolved Design section: 'Keep the canonical portable rebar-usage prose in ONE source (natural candidate: docs/ticket-model.md plus the workflow loop authored in S1, or a dedicated docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md)'
scenarios: Executor creates docs/ticket-model.md (one choice), writes template against it, test passes — but a second executor picks docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md, creating two divergent implementations from the same plan.
If the 're-home story' (named as a dependency) is not resolved before implementation, the canonical source content itself is unsettled, meaning the template content cannot be finalized either.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a concrete deliverable: 'Create `docs/_shared/rebar-usage.md` as the canonical portable rebar-usage prose; `CANONICAL` in `tests/unit/test_client_template_sync.py` is wired to this path.' Remove the 'natural candidate' hedge and resolve the dependency on the re-home story before closing this plan.
